claimText: The National Bioenergy Programme (NBP) provides an additional 20% Central Financial Assistance (CFA) for special categories, including the North East Region, hilly states, and SC/ST beneficiaries.
evidenceExcerpt: The National Bioenergy Programme provides 20% more Central Financial Assistance (CFA) for special categories like the North East Region, hilly states, and SC/ST beneficiaries.
examUse: Statement-based question on the financial incentives and target beneficiaries of the National Bioenergy Programme.
distractorHooks: Excludes hilly states from special categories

claimText: The Waste to Energy component of the National Bioenergy Programme supports energy generation through advanced technologies such as pyrolysis and plasma gasification.
evidenceExcerpt: The Waste to Energy Programme supports projects generating Biogas, BioCNG, Power, or Syngas through technologies like pyrolysis and plasma gasification.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the specific technologies approved under the Waste to Energy Programme.

claimText: Biogas is primarily composed of 95% methane (CH4) and carbon dioxide (CO2), along with trace amounts of other gases like hydrogen sulfide (H2S).
evidenceExcerpt: Biogas is composed of 95% methane (CH4) and CO2, with traces of N2, H2, H2S, and O2.
examUse: Statement-based question on the chemical composition of biogas.
distractorHooks: Primarily composed of nitrogen and oxygen

claimText: The revised guidelines for the Waste to Energy Programme mandate a two-stage release of Central Financial Assistance, with the first 50% released after obtaining State Pollution Control Board consent.
evidenceExcerpt: Revised guidelines for Waste to Energy introduce a two-stage CFA release: 50% after State Pollution Control Board consent and the remainder after 80% capacity achievement.
examUse: Statement-based question on the implementation and fund disbursal mechanisms of the Waste to Energy Programme.
distractorHooks: Requires Central Pollution Control Board consent instead of State
Second installment released immediately after project commissioning

claimText: Under the Biomass Programme, pellet producers in the National Capital Region (NCR) have the option to choose between schemes from the MNRE or the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) for stubble burning mitigation.
evidenceExcerpt: The Biomass Programme supports stubble burning mitigation by allowing pellet producers in NCR to choose between MNRE or CPCB schemes.
examUse: Statement-based question on institutional frameworks and options available for stubble burning mitigation in the NCR.
distractorHooks: Mandatory to use only the MNRE scheme
Only applicable to farmers in Southern India
